prerequisites: A Discord account with access to the Discord Developer Portal (https://discord.com/developers/applications).
A registered Discord application with a bot user, which provides the bot token used for the required bot_token field.
The bot added to each Discord server (guild) you want to read data from, since bots can only access guilds they have joined.

authMethods.name: Bot token
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a Discord bot token supplied in the required bot_token field. Discord authorizes requests via the Authorization HTTP header using the literal prefix Bot followed by a space and the token (for example, 'Authorization: Bot <token>'). The token is generated and reset from the Bot page of your application in the Discord Developer Portal.

setupSteps.title: Create a Discord application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Discord Developer Portal at https://discord.com/developers/applications and create a new application. Registering an application also provisions an associated bot user.

setupSteps.title: Get the bot token
setupSteps.body: Open your application's Bot settings page in the Developer Portal. Copy the bot token (reset it first if it has never been revealed). This value is the bot_token the connector requires. Treat it as a secret, since anyone holding it can act as your bot.

setupSteps.title: Add the bot to your server(s)
setupSteps.body: Bots join servers through Discord's OAuth2 authorization flow rather than normal invites. Build an authorization URL with the bot scope, your application's client_id, and a permissions integer (for example, https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=<your_client_id>&scope=bot&permissions=<permissions>), open it, and add the bot to each guild you have permission to manage. The bot can only read data from guilds it has joined.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the bot token into the required bot_token field of the source-discord connector configuration. Discord enforces a global rate limit of about 50 requests per second plus per-route limits, returning HTTP 429 with a retry_after value when exceeded, so expect throttling on large syncs.

sources.title: Discord API Reference (authentication and Authorization: Bot header)
Discord OAuth2 (creating an app, bot token, adding a bot to a guild)
